hook.text: On April 26, 1986, operators at Chernobyl ran a safety test. It triggered the worst nuclear disaster ever.

buildup.text: The test required disabling multiple safety systems. The operators followed orders despite their unease.

discovery.text: A sudden power surge caused a steam explosion that blew the 1,000-ton reactor lid off the building.

twist.text: Soviet officials denied the disaster for two days — until Swedish sensors detected radiation 1,000 km away.

climax.text: 350,000 people were permanently evacuated. The area remains uninhabitable nearly 40 years later.

punchline.text: A safety test caused the world's worst safety disaster.

quiz.question: How did the Chernobyl disaster begin?
options: A safety test went catastrophically wrong
A missile struck the power plant
